description_long: This sculpture features a detailed, stylized figure that appears to be inspired by Mesoamerican or Aztec motifs, with a prominent headdress and intricate facial features. The figure is carved in high relief from a block of stone, with the upper portion displaying the ornate head and limbs, while the lower portion is a more geometric, rectangular base. The surface of the upper section is finely detailed, with visible textural contrasts between the smooth and rougher areas. The base is divided into two distinct textures: a rough, dark stone upper half and a smoother, lighter stone lower half, possibly indicating restoration or mounting. There is a faint engraving or plaque visible on the lower front face of the base. The overall form is monolithic and imposing, suggesting it is intended as a public monument or decorative urban feature.
description: A monolithic stone sculpture with a detailed, stylized figure atop a rectangular base, featuring both rough and smooth stone textures and a visible plaque on the lower section.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the intricate facial features and headdress of the figure, as well as the plaque on the lower base.
description_view_1: The rear view shows the rough, uncarved stone surface and the geometric shape of the base, with minimal detail on the figure.
description_view_2: The side view reveals the transition between the rough and smooth textures of the base and the profile of the headdress.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the detailed carving of the figure and the contrast between the ornate upper section and the plain base.
attribute_subject_matter: stylized Mesoamerican or Aztec figure with headdress
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be displayed in an indoor or controlled environment, possibly a museum or gallery, as suggested by the clean base and visible plaque.
